title: centaurea seusana var. lingulata
intro: Centaurea seusana var. lingulata, also known as a variety of Knapweed, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its blue-purple flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea seusana var. lingulata are believed to include relief from digestive issues, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, are often used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions.
